question_text: A Sankey diagram shows energy transfers in a light bulb. What does the width of the arrow represent?

options.label: A
options.text: The time taken for the energy transfer

options.label: B
options.text: The amount of energy being transferred

options.label: D
options.text: The speed at which energy moves

correct_answer: B) The amount of energy being transferred
explanation: In a Sankey diagram, the width of each arrow is proportional to the quantity of energy involved. A thicker arrow means more energy, and a thinner arrow means less energy, making it easy to see where energy goes in a system.
